channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Démarrage lent et RAM qui fuit : Microsoft corrige trois bugs de Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft avait promis que 2026 serait l'année de la fiabilité pour Windows 11. La mise à jour KB5083631 commence à donner raison aux optimistes.

item.title: Microsoft’s Office and LinkedIn chief now runs Teams in latest reshuffle
item.description: Microsoft's LinkedIn CEO, Ryan Roslansky, took on an expanded role at the company as head of Office last year, and he's now getting more responsibilities as part of the latest leadership reshuffle inside Microsoft. Sources tell me that the Microsoft Teams organization is moving to report to Roslansky, who will now lead a new Work […]

item.title: 12+ compact accessories that'll turn your phone into the perfect video and photo tool for travel — from $10 / £10
item.description: Travel-friendly extras to instantly level up your camera phone.

item.title: Opel Corsa GSE : avec 281 ch, l’Alpine A290 a-t-elle enfin trouvé sa vraie rivale ?
item.description: Opel sort de ses cartons la Corsa GSE. Version pimentée de la Corsa électrique, la GSE fait grimper la puissance à 281 ch. Les performances s’en ressentent, alors que l’allemande se présente sous un package légèrement différenciant.

item.title: Microsoft vows to make Windows 11's File Explorer much faster — and it's about time
item.description: Fed up with File Explorer running sluggishly in Windows 11? Microsoft's doing 'foundational' work to fully fix it.

item.title: Best Indoor Security Cameras (2026): For Homes and Apartments
item.description: Cameras can offer peace of mind, but choose carefully before inviting one into your home.

item.title: Regardez le X-59 de la Nasa en plein test : ce supersonique qui veut effacer le bang du mur du son
item.description: Le dernier vol de l'avion X-59 de la Nasa a eu lieu récemment. Son but : réaliser des tests proches de la vitesse du son tout en étant le plus silencieux possible. Bientôt, il volera près d'endroits habités.

item.title: 'Trust is not keeping pace with technological capability': New study finds people are becoming more accepting of AI - but don't want to hand over full control just yet
item.description: AI can get a lot done, but companies are actually struggling to trust it and legacy identity management software doesn't quite cut it.

item.title: Best Webcams (2026): My Honest Take After Testing the Best
item.description: I tested all the major webcams across the price spectrum in attempts to find the very best. Here's what I learned.

item.title: The excellent Bose QuietComfort Earbuds are back down to their Black Friday price
item.description: The Bose QuietComfort Earbuds are a great pair of earbuds that just got even cheaper thanks to this Amazon deal.

item.title: The clippening
item.description: Earlier this year, after a tumultuous period serving as the former second-in-command at the FBI, Dan Bongino went back to what he is perhaps known best for: video podcasting. After Bongino exited the role in January, he began promotion for the return of his podcast, The Dan Bongino Show. He bought out a billboard in […]

item.title: The Pentagon wants lasers. Can anyone build them fast enough?
item.description: The military wants ‘tens to hundreds’ of laser weapons. After years of prototypes, the real challenge is scaling production. This article is republished with permission from Laser Wars, a newsletter about military laser weapons and other futuristic defense technology.

item.title: Vie numérique | L’IA canadienne devient la « troisième voie » après la Chine et les États-Unis
item.description: L’IA canadienne s’impose à l’international. La prise de possession par la torontoise Cohere de son homologue allemande Aleph Alpha crée une troisième voie mondiale derrière l’IA chinoise et états-unienne, prête à offrir une technologie « responsable, privée et sûre ».

item.title: Le site officiel de Daemon Tools distribue un malware capable de contrôler votre Windows
item.description: Le logiciel Daemon Tools a été victime d'une attaque de la chaîne d'approvisionnement. Depuis le 8 avril 2026, des versions malveillantes du programme sont distribuées via le site officiel. Elles intègrent une porte dérobée capable de contrôler les machines infectées.

item.title: 'Younger job‑seekers aren't being careless, they're under pressure': LinkedIn study flags risks facing younger job-hunters today
item.description: Scammers are targeting prospective workers with dodgy job postings – younger workers might actually be most at risk.

item.title: Apple Will Pay $250 Million to Settle Lawsuit Over Siri's AI Features
item.description: If you bought an iPhone 15 or 16 in the US, you could be set to pocket up to $95 per device as part of the settlement.

item.title: Do not interrupt Hannah Waddinghams SNL UK vocal warm-up
item.description: "Ted Lasso" star and West End icon Hannah Waddingham is hosting "SNL UK" on May 9 and the teaser promises perfection.

item.title: Microsoft has finally realized what most of us knew all along: nobody actually wanted Copilot on Xbox
item.description: Xbox is no longer developing Copilot for consoles.

item.title: Utah’s VPN crackdown has arrived: here are the VPNs we recommend to protect your personal data and privacy online in the beehive state
item.description: Utah is pioneering controversial new internet restrictions, making a reliable VPN essential for digital privacy. Here are the top three providers offering the best speeds, local servers, and security for residents of the Beehive State.

item.title: The Philips Norelco i9000 Prestige Ultra Day & Night delivers flagship rotary shaving with a sci-fi twist
item.description: This flagship shaver from Philips is smooth, smart, and slightly over-engineered.

item.title: Focal Mu-So Hekla Soundbar Review: Dolby Atmos With 1 Speaker
item.description: The Focal Mu-so Hekla can almost replace a multi-speaker surround-sound setup.

item.title: Whataburger’s redesigned packaging proves the Happy Meal could be happier
item.description: ‘We wanted to build something that was a bit more intentional and experience-led.’ —Whataburger CMO Scott Hudler Whataburger is rethinking the fast-food kids meal.

item.title: PlayStation users could get paid from a $7.85 million settlement. Here’s who qualifies
item.description: Millions are included automatically, but the fine print will decide who gets what. A yearslong battle between Sony PlayStation and its customers might soon be coming to an end after the approval of a preliminary settlement agreement for a class-action lawsuit on April 29.

item.title: Best Water Leak Detectors (2026): Moen, Phyn, TP-Link
item.description: Don’t let busted pipes or an overflowing washing machine dampen your day. These smart water sensors can help you catch problems quicker.

item.title: How AI's evolution is redefining risks
item.description: Industrialized cybercrime means AI has moved from a double-edged to a triple-edged sword.

item.title: Garmin has just launched super-elite hi-fi gear, through its sub-brand that's best-known for its maritime sound systems — and the stereo speakers are priced (perhaps appropriately) like a pair of Bowers & Wilkins' Nautilus
item.description: Garmin has unveiled some hi-fi speakers through its JL Audio brand, including a streamer, loudspeakers and floor speakers.

item.title: 35 % de remise pour cette puissante barre de son JBL équipée d’enceintes détachables et d’un caisson de basses
item.description: Avec son design original composé d'enceintes détachables, la barre de son JBL Bar 1000MK2 devient un véritable système audio polyvalent. En ce moment, elle est à découvrir pour 415 euros de moins chez Amazon.

item.title: The Samsung G9 Odyssey gaming monitor is $300 off at Amazon — score a free code for Resident Evil Requiem
item.description: Save $300 on Samsung’s 49-inch Odyssey G9 at Amazon and get a free Resident Evil Requiem PC code.

item.title: Chrome’s AI features may be hogging 4GB of your computer storage
item.description: Google Chrome may be taking up more of your storage than expected thanks to a large on-device AI model file that, in some cases, is being automatically downloaded to the browser's system folders. Users who have noticed unexplained drops in their available desktop device storage are now discovering that Chrome is installing a 4GB weights.bin […]

item.title: Le « moment Yaris » : comment la Chine copie la recette de Toyota pour envahir l’Europe de ses voitures électriques bon marché
item.description: L'industrie automobile chinoise vient de changer radicalement de stratégie pour conquérir l'Europe. Fini les immenses SUV inadaptés à nos routes, les constructeurs misent désormais sur des citadines électriques pensées pour nous. Mais attention, avoir le bon format ne garantit pas un succès immédiat en Europe.

item.title: « La pression est énorme » : le plan secret de Ford pour créer une voiture électrique abordable face au raz-de-marée chinois
item.description: Pour regagner en compétitivité et pouvoir se battre contre la Chine, Ford développe une nouvelle plateforme dédiée à des voitures électriques abordables dans un centre dédié, que plusieurs journalistes ont pu visiter. De quoi mieux comprendre leur fonctionnement inédit pour la marque.

item.title: Chrome installe en douce un modèle d’IA de 4 Go sur votre ordinateur, sans rien demander
item.description: Un fichier de 4 Go nommé weights.bin atterrit sur les ordinateurs des utilisateurs de Chrome sans avertissement, sans case à cocher, sans bouton de désinstallation. Et si on le supprime, le navigateur le retélécharge tout seul.

item.title: Smeg Forte Stand Mixer Review: Big Power, Mixed Results
item.description: Smeg’s new retro-looking Forte stand mixer packs some serious power, but its performance isn’t as polished as its design.

item.title: How Substack became the new book tour
item.description: Lena Dunham and other writers are using Substack to reach readers directly and sell more books. It’s hard enough to publish a book, but getting people to buy it is an entirely different battle. As new platforms reshape how readers gather and interact online, authors are finding that sometimes platforms built to showcase writing can also double as powerful engines for discovery....

item.title: 7 of the biggest names in AI, including OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, and Amazon, are now moving into military systems — but one major player is missing from the list, and that raises uncomfortable questions about where this technology is heading
item.description: A sweeping Pentagon push to integrate AI into military systems includes nearly every major tech player except Anthropic, exposing a growing divide over how far such technology should go.

item.title: Hasan Piker, Self-Described ‘Ayatollah of Woke,’ Wants AI to Die
item.description: The far-left Twitch streamer says AI is rotting our brains. He’s also addicted to Twitter and listens to at least eight podcasts.

item.title: Target’s new shopping cart is built for Stanleys and Starbucks (exclusive)
item.description: Big. Red. And all plastic. Target’s new shopping carts are beefier than ever to make your trip through the store even more comfortable. It’s the three-row SUV of big-box retail. Target’s bold red shopping cart has always anchored customers inside a Target store, promising a middle-class fancy experience.

item.title: Why women are looking for jobs for their unemployed husbands
item.description: It’s a trend becoming more apparent across the internet, rooted in longstanding gender differences and expectations. No matter who you are, searching for work while unemployed is a difficult, sometimes soul-crushing endeavor. Across the country, job seekers are desperately looking for ways to stand out in an increasingly competitive job market as AI complicates the search process and career boards fill up with nonexistent “ghost jobs.” ...

item.title: Who is Peter Arnell, America’s new chief brand architect?
item.description: Who is Peter Arnell, America’s new chief brand architect? You might know him from Tropicana’s failed rebrand or that infamous Mike Tyson Super Bowl ad. The Trump administration just elected designer Peter Arnell “chief brand architect” of the National Design Studio. It’s a role that could have massive ripple effects for how the American government presents itself. ...

item.title: Stephen Colbert challenges Barack Obama to a tense wastepaper basketball rematch
item.description: Stephen Colbert and Barack Obama have taken part in a "wastepaper basketball" rematch.

item.title: Whoops! Samsung just leaked its Galaxy Wide Fold and Z Fold 8 designs — and one is much more interesting than the other
item.description: Images of the Samsung Galaxy Wide Fold and Z Fold 8 have both been spotted in Samsung code.

item.title: Steam Controller : Valve publie les fichiers 3D pour personnaliser sa manette
item.description: Valve facilite la personnalisation et le bidouillage de son Steam Controller en mettant à disposition tous les fichiers de design pour sa manette PC.

item.title: iPhone 17e : deux mois après sa sortie, son prix est enfin en baisse [Sponso]
item.description: Deux mois seulement, c'est le temps qu'il a fallu à Bouygues Telecom pour baisser le prix de l'iPhone 17e après sa sortie, avec un généreux forfait mobile de 250 Go.
